response:
The living room LED function is defined as \( L(t) = 500 - N(t) \), which simplifies to \( L(t) = 200 - 200\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{12}t\right) \) lumens for times when \( N(t) < 500 \), and \( L(t) = 0 \) when natural light exceeds the target; integrating this over 24 hours with the given power rate yields a total energy consumption of 480 watt-hours. The bedroom color combinations form a continuous set of points on the plane \( 0.3r + 0.6g + 0.1b = 500 \) within the cube \( [0, 255]^3 \), representing all valid RGB triplets that satisfy the lumen requirement.
